name: Camazotz
description: Camazotz, meaning 'death bat' in K'iche' Mayan, is a fearsome bat-god associated with the night, death, and sacrifice. He is a prominent figure in Mayan mythology, particularly in the Popol Vuh, where he plays a significant role in the trials faced by the Maya Hero Twins, Hunahpu and Xbalanque, in the underworld of Xibalba. Camazotz dwells in the House of Bats, a dangerous chamber filled with swarms of these creatures. He is often depicted with a humanoid body, the head of a bat, and sharp claws, embodying terror and representing the dangers lurking in the darkness. His existence serves as a warning against venturing into the unknown and the respect that should be given to the forces of nature and the underworld. He is a powerful symbol of the fearsome aspects of the Mayan cosmos, demonstrating the importance of balance between life and death, light and darkness. His image is often used to invoke fear and respect for the power of the underworld rulers.
subtitle: The Death Bat of Mayan Legend
appearance: Camazotz is typically depicted as a humanoid figure with the head of a bat. He possesses large, leathery wings, sharp claws, and piercing eyes that glow in the dark. His teeth are often portrayed as being razor-sharp, capable of tearing flesh. He is often adorned with symbols of death and sacrifice, further emphasizing his connection to the underworld.
habitat: Camazotz dwells in caves, particularly the House of Bats in Xibalba, the Mayan underworld. These caves are often dark, damp, and filled with the sounds of fluttering wings, creating an atmosphere of fear and dread.
behavior: Camazotz is a creature of the night, preying on those who are foolish enough to venture into his domain. He is aggressive, territorial, and fiercely protective of his home. He embodies the dangers of the underworld and serves as a guardian of the boundary between the world of the living and the realm of the dead.

keyMyths.title: The House of Bats
keyMyths.description: In the Popol Vuh, Hunahpu and Xbalanque enter the House of Bats, where they are surrounded by thousands of Camazotz. Hunahpu is decapitated by one of the bats while waiting for sunrise.

sources.author: Dennis Tedlock
sources.description: A modern translation and commentary on the Popol Vuh.
sources.text: A modern translation and commentary on the Popol Vuh.
